title: Browser Approval Submission Packet Design
objective: Author the governed design bridge from the read-only PM intake workbench to a later explicitly admitted browser approval submission lane, defining the payload, UI confirmation, success/failure handling, idempotent replay, readback proof, and first approval-row execution gate without sending a live POST or creating an approval row.
active_role: Technical Repo Authority / PM Lane Coordinator / Browser Approval Submission Design Author

source_floor.meaning: Current clean-main after PM Lane 140 reconciled the PM intake workbench with hosted approval-readiness truth while preserving zero browser approval/import mutation authority.

allowed_scope: Author the browser approval submission design and future execution gate requirements.
Define the exact approval persistence envelope and payload fields already required by apps/mutation-seam.
Define PM-facing confirmation copy, blocked-state copy, success-state copy, failure-state copy, idempotent replay handling, and status-readback expectations for a later UI implementation lane.
Define the minimum acceptance evidence for the future first approval-row creation lane.
Update PM operating docs, status, packet, and handoff surfaces for the no-write design lane.
Use read-only sidecar scout output as recommendation context.
not_allowed: No project, workpackage, task, apparatus, issue, assignment, schedule, status, durable field record, production tracking, workbook, or import rows.
No app product code changes in this design lane.
No live Supabase SQL application or schema migration.
No Render, Vercel, Olares, or Supabase action.
No secret value access, print, rotation, or repo storage.
No workbook macro execution or workbook writeback.
No service creation, DNS/auth/ingress change, fixture replay into live data, work authorization, field release, live work order creation, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.

required_envelope.idempotency_key: Must equal payload.idempotency_key from the current approval contract.
required_envelope.entity_id: Must be omitted or equal the deterministic approval_record_id produced from candidate_id, candidate_version, source_stat_fingerprint, candidate_shape_fingerprint, and idempotency_key.
required_envelope.payload: Must contain the required approval payload fields listed below.
required_envelope.reason: Must clearly state this persists only the PM import-candidate approval record and does not import rows.
required_envelope.client_timestamp: Browser timestamp for submission evidence; server remains source of approved_at_utc.

payload_rules: decision must be one of approve_for_import_packet, return_for_revision, or reject_candidate.
accepted_warning_codes must exactly match the current candidate warning-code set from the approval contract.
accepted_no_go_overrides must exactly match the approval contract's needs_human_acceptance check ids.
PM actor authority comes from the authenticated token; the backend normalizes approved_by_actor_id and approved_at_utc.
offline_queue source is rejected for this Class C approval persistence path.
ui_confirmation_requirements: Require an explicit PM confirmation control before any future POST is enabled.
Show candidate id, candidate version, source fingerprint, shape fingerprint, decision value, warning-code acceptance, no-go acknowledgement, and review notes in the confirmation view.

Warn that approval persistence is not project import authority and does not create workpackages, tasks, apparatus, assignments, schedules, statuses, field records, work orders, or production tracking rows.
Keep project import controls absent after approval persistence succeeds until a separate import packet admits that mutation.
future_success_evidence: HTTP 200 accepted or idempotent_hit response from POST /api/v1/mutations/project-import-approvals.
Response entity_type is pm_import_candidate_approval and action_type is persist_import_approval.
GET /api/v1/reads/project-import-approval-status returns approved_for_import_packet, returned_for_revision, or rejected_candidate as appropriate for the same candidate.
Approval record count changes from 0 to 1 only in the explicitly admitted first-row execution lane, or remains 1 on idempotent replay.
Project, workpackage, task, apparatus, assignment, schedule, status, durable field record, and production tracking counts remain unchanged.
future_failure_handling: Reject stale candidate/source/shape/idempotency values and return the PM to source review.
Reject warning-code mismatches and require renewed warning acceptance.
Reject missing or mismatched human-acceptance no-go acknowledgements.
Reject unsupported decisions and empty review notes.
Treat idempotency duplicate with mismatched payload as a blocker, not as success.
Show storage-unavailable readback as a blocker and do not proceed to import.

validation_steps: rg -n "PM Lane 141|No live approval POST|No approval row|not_admitted|Browser Approval Submission Packet Design" PROJECT_STATUS.md docs/operations ops/agents
validation_result: PASS: packet JSON parsed with ConvertFrom-Json; scoped guardrail/status rg returned PM Lane 141, no-live-POST, no-approval-row, not_admitted, and Browser Approval Submission Packet Design references; scoped git diff --check passed.
sidecar_result: Read-only sidecar recommended PM Lane 141 as a browser approval submission design/packet lane, not a live submission lane. It identified the exact likely files, guardrails, and validation commands, and recommended a separate explicit execution lane for first approval-row creation.
next_recommended_lane: PM Lane 142 - Browser Approval Submission First-Row Execution Gate, only after explicit stakeholder admission of live POST and first approval-row creation
